item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MTF Alpha-18 (Wrap it before you tap it)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had already decided this was interesting by the time I realized it was a giant rehashing of sexual fertilization. (And a biologically informed one at that, with the theorized head of the SCP-3278-2s being damaged upon entry, a majority of them dying of natural causes before ever reaching SCP-3278, etc.) That took it from well-done to awesome. My favorite line happens to be unrelated, but is: "of which there is a non-anomalously enlarged local population".

Also, sucks for the research staff; they are situated right in the middle of extraterrestrial sperm showers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, it's got a good sense of mystery in the whole thing and it's paced well enough that you give people plenty of space to work out what it is by themselves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The results of entry are completely unknown."

I don't know nuthin' bout makin' no babies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can fuck with the idea of an unfertilized Angel egg.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In most cases, multiple instances of SCP-3278-2 survive the descent, and jostle for entry.

This is where I figured it out. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If an MTF squad is ever assigned to protect SCP-3278 and/or prevent instances of SCP-3278-1 from entering SCP-3278, their nickname damn well better have something to do with condoms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a perfectly valid criticism, but in my head-canon they definitely see the similarities, but aren't saying it for fear of mis-applying human logic to an anomalous phenomenon. The final line indicates that they suspect the way things might go down.

If you have any suggestions about how to better implement this idea without breaking the reveal, I would greatly appreciate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's space sperm trying to fertilize an earth egg.

I'm not sure if the researchers' inability to note the similarities there is breaking my disbelief or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here it is, hope you like! Thanks to Captain Kirby, DalaiLlama, and Leveritas for all of their insightful feedback.

In addition, thanks to all site staff and veterans for continued maintenance and leadership. Your dedication is greatly appreciated.
